hero.headline: Dr. MGR Online MBA in Marketing Management: Fees, Syllabus and Career Scope
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme | NAAC A+ accredited | Rs 1,36,000 - Rs 1,41,000 total fee | Deemed University since 2003

sections.tldr: Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute Online MBA in Marketing Management covers Advertising and Sales Promotion and Services Marketing in Sem 3, plus Rural Marketing in Sem 4. Common core includes Strategic Management, Entrepreneurship Development, Digital Marketing, International Business Management, and an 8-week mandatory Field Work and Project Work. Total fee Rs 1,36,000 to Rs 1,41,000 across 4 semesters. NAAC A+ accredited, UGC-DEB approved, Deemed University.

about.body:
Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute is a Deemed University in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, named after former Chief Minister Dr. M.G. Ramachandran. The institution has operated under UGC recognition since 2003 and holds NAAC A+ accreditation with UGC-DEB approval for online programmes.

The Marketing Management specialisation covers Advertising and Sales Promotion and Services Marketing in Sem 3, with Rural Marketing as a Sem 4 spec-specific subject. The inclusion of Rural Marketing is relevant for Tamil Nadu and South India markets, where rural and semi-urban consumer segments drive significant FMCG, agri-business, and financial services demand.

The common Sem 4 core adds Digital Marketing as a complementary subject, giving marketing students both traditional (advertising, services, rural) and digital marketing coverage. The 8-week field project can be completed in a marketing department, ad agency, or FMCG distribution setup.

At Rs 1,36,000 to Rs 1,41,000 total, the programme sits in the mid-range for NAAC A+ online MBAs. Chennai's presence as a major FMCG, automotive, and services marketing hub adds regional career relevance.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Marketing Management specialisation subjects. Spec-specific subjects listed first, followed by common core.

subjects.name: Advertising and Sales Promotion
subjects.desc: Advertising strategy, media planning, creative brief development, sales promotion tools, IMC (Integrated Marketing Communications), and ad effectiveness measurement.

subjects.name: Services Marketing
subjects.desc: Service quality models (SERVQUAL), service blueprinting, customer experience design, hospitality and healthcare services marketing, and service recovery strategies.

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.desc: Competitive strategy frameworks, industry analysis, corporate-level strategy, and strategic implementation processes.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship Development
subjects.desc: Startup ideation, D2C brand building, funding strategies, and MSME support systems for marketing ventures.

subjects.name: Management Accounting
subjects.desc: Cost-volume-profit analysis, marketing budget allocation, variance analysis, and management decision-making tools.

subjects.name: Production and Operations Management
subjects.desc: Production planning, quality management, supply chain basics, and operations scheduling.

subjects.name: Skill Development
subjects.desc: Communication skills, presentation techniques, negotiation skills, and professional development modules.

subjects.name: Summer Project
subjects.desc: Industry-linked summer project in a marketing function requiring market research, data collection, and structured report.

subjects.name: Rural Marketing
subjects.desc: Rural consumer behaviour, distribution strategies for tier-3/4 markets, ITC e-Choupal model, FMCG rural penetration, and agri-business marketing.

subjects.name: Digital Marketing
subjects.desc: SEO, SEM, social media marketing, content strategy, email marketing, influencer marketing, and digital analytics tools.

subjects.name: International Business Management
subjects.desc: Global marketing strategy, export marketing, cross-cultural consumer behaviour, and international brand management.

subjects.name: Contemporary Seminar
subjects.desc: Student-led seminar on current marketing topics. Research, presentation, and peer discussion on emerging marketing trends.

subjects.name: Field Work and Project Work (8 Weeks)
subjects.desc: Mandatory 8-week field project in a marketing department, agency, or distribution setup. Structured report, data analysis, and viva voce defence.

whoHires.body:
Dr. MGR MBA Marketing Management graduates target roles across advertising, FMCG, services, and digital marketing:

**Industries:** FMCG, automotive, retail, e-commerce, advertising agencies, media houses, banking (retail marketing), healthcare marketing, telecom, and IT services marketing

**Entry-level roles:** Marketing Executive, Brand Associate, Sales Officer, Digital Marketing Coordinator, Media Planner (junior), Market Research Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years):** Brand Manager, Area Sales Manager, Digital Marketing Manager, Product Manager, Services Marketing Manager, Rural Sales Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Marketing, Chief Marketing Officer (mid-market), Head of Brand, National Sales Head, Director of Digital Marketing

**Employers:** FMCG companies (ITC, HUL, P&G, Nestle), automotive (Hyundai Chennai plant, TVS, Royal Enfield), IT services marketing (TCS, Infosys), advertising agencies, and South India retail chains.

**Note:** Chennai is home to major FMCG distribution hubs and the Hyundai/TVS automotive corridor. Dr. MGR's local brand provides access to the Tamil Nadu marketing job market.

skills.body:
The Marketing Management specialisation builds skills across advertising, services marketing, and rural markets:

**Technical skills:**
- Advertising: media planning and buying, creative strategy development, IMC campaign design, ad effectiveness metrics (reach, frequency, GRPs), and sales promotion mechanics
- Services Marketing: SERVQUAL application, service blueprinting, customer journey mapping, service recovery design, and Net Promoter Score management
- Rural Marketing: rural distribution strategy (hub-and-spoke), rural consumer profiling, sachet pricing, mandi-based distribution, and agri-input marketing

**Applied skills:**
- Digital Marketing (from common core): SEO/SEM execution, social media campaign management, Google Analytics interpretation, and content marketing strategy
- Market Research: survey design, focus group facilitation, data analysis for marketing decisions, and competitive intelligence gathering

**Soft skills:** Client presentation, brand storytelling, negotiating media rates, managing agency relationships, and pitching marketing plans to leadership.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Dr. MGR Online MBA Marketing Management against peer programmes:

**vs Vignan Online MBA Marketing (Rs 90,000):** Vignan is Rs 46,000 to Rs 51,000 cheaper. Both cover marketing specialisation subjects. Dr. MGR has advertising/sales promotion, services marketing, and rural marketing as dedicated subjects. Vignan wins on fee. Dr. MGR wins on rural marketing depth and Chennai FMCG/automotive market access.

**vs SMU Online MBA Marketing (Rs 1,20,000):** SMU is Rs 16,000 to Rs 21,000 cheaper with the Manipal group brand. SMU has established pan-India placement infrastructure. Dr. MGR has NAAC A+ (same tier), rural marketing as a standalone subject, and the 8-week field project. SMU wins on brand. Dr. MGR wins on project depth.

**vs Galgotias Online MBA Marketing (Rs 86,400):** Galgotias is Rs 49,600 to Rs 54,600 cheaper. Both are NAAC A+. Galgotias has NCR location for North India FMCG/retail access. Dr. MGR has rural marketing depth and South India market access. Choose based on regional career focus and fee priority.

fees.body:
The Dr. MGR Online MBA in Marketing Management is priced between Rs 1,36,000 and Rs 1,41,000 total across 4 semesters (2-year programme). Fee range varies slightly by specialization - contact our counsellor for the exact figure for your chosen track.

**Payment breakdown**:
- **Per semester**: Rs 34,000 - Rs 40,000 across 4 semesters
- **Full upfront option**: Rs 1,36,000 - Rs 1,41,000 one-time

**Additional charges**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time, non-refundable at admission)

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute starting Rs 5,700 per month. No external marketing partner or NBFC intermediary.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Dr. MGR Online each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees for Marketing Management with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Marketing Management as the specialisation, issued by Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute (Deemed University) under UGC-DEB online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A+ accreditation, UGC-DEB approval, and Deemed University status since 2003. Valid for marketing manager roles, government recruitment, PSU marketing positions, and private-sector brand/sales management careers. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from Dr. MGR Online MBA Marketing Management students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Anitha
items.liked: Advertising and Sales Promotion covered media planning and IMC at a practical level useful for my role at a Chennai ad agency. The brand carries weight in Tamil Nadu
items.disliked: Services Marketing could include more digital service design content. The subject felt traditional
items.body: I chose Dr. MGR because Chennai ad agencies and FMCG companies recognize the name. NAAC A+ at this fee is fair. Advertising and Sales Promotion was directly relevant. Rural Marketing in Sem 4 was an unexpected strength, covering ITC e-Choupal and sachet pricing models. The 8-week field project at my agency added real portfolio value. For TN marketing careers, Dr. MGR is a solid pick.

items.name: Kumar
items.liked: Rural Marketing as a standalone subject is rare in online MBAs. Directly useful for my FMCG distribution role in semi-urban Tamil Nadu
items.disliked: National brand recognition outside South India is limited. Mumbai or Delhi FMCG employers may not know Dr. MGR
items.body: The marketing curriculum covers traditional (advertising, services, rural) and digital marketing across Sem 3-4. Three spec-specific subjects plus Digital Marketing in common core give four marketing-relevant courses. The 8-week project was the highlight. At Rs 1.36-1.41L, the fee is mid-range. Vignan and Galgotias are cheaper but lack the rural marketing and Tamil Nadu brand advantage.

items.name: Nandini
items.liked: Services Marketing covered SERVQUAL and service blueprinting at a depth useful for my hospitality marketing role
items.disliked: LMS could be more interactive. Some recorded sessions feel one-directional. Faculty engagement varies by subject
items.body: At Rs 1.36-1.41L, Dr. MGR is not the cheapest marketing MBA. Galgotias at Rs 86,400 and Vignan at Rs 90,000 are significantly less. Dr. MGR's value is the Tamil Nadu brand and rural marketing depth. For South India FMCG and automotive marketing careers, the regional recognition helps. For pan-India brand management roles at top FMCG firms, Manipal or Amity have stronger national brands.

faqs.question: Is Dr. MGR a Deemed University?
faqs.answer: Yes. Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute has operated as a Deemed University under UGC recognition since 2003. Named after former Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Dr. M.G. Ramachandran.

faqs.question: What is the fee difference between Rs 1.36L and Rs 1.41L?
faqs.answer: The total fee varies slightly by specialization. Contact our counsellor to confirm the exact fee for Marketing Management before making any payment.

faqs.question: Is Dr. MGR Online MBA UGC-DEB approved?
faqs.answer: Yes. Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute is UGC-DEB approved for online programmes. The degree is legally equivalent to a campus MBA under UGC 2020 guidelines.

faqs.question: Why does the Marketing MBA include Rural Marketing?
faqs.answer: Rural Marketing is a Sem 4 spec-specific subject covering rural consumer behaviour, tier-3/4 distribution strategies, sachet pricing, and agri-business marketing. This is particularly relevant for FMCG, agri-input, and financial services companies targeting South India rural markets.

faqs.question: Does the programme cover Digital Marketing too?
faqs.answer: Yes. Digital Marketing is a common Sem 4 core subject covering SEO, SEM, social media, and analytics. Combined with the spec-specific Advertising and Sales Promotion, Services Marketing, and Rural Marketing, marketing students get both traditional and digital coverage.

chooseIf: You are based in Tamil Nadu or South India where Dr. MGR's brand carries strong recognition in FMCG, automotive, and services sectors.
Rural Marketing as a dedicated subject aligns with your career in semi-urban or rural market distribution.
NAAC A+ at a mid-range fee (Rs 1.36-1.41L) meets your requirements.
The 8-week mandatory field project in a marketing department or agency is valuable for portfolio building.
consider: Vignan at Rs 90,000 or Galgotias at Rs 86,400 offer marketing MBAs at significantly lower fees with NAAC A+.
SMU at Rs 1,20,000 has stronger pan-India brand recognition with the Manipal group backing.
For Mumbai/Delhi FMCG marketing careers, Dr. MGR's regional brand may not carry sufficient weight.
If digital marketing depth is your priority over rural/traditional marketing, compare syllabi with Manipal or Amity.
